questions.stem:
—I ______ the book already. 
—Oh, really? When ______ you ______ it?
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。第一空后有already（已经），是现在完成时的标志词，结构为have/has+过去分词，故用have read；第二空询问过去发生的动作时间，用一般过去时的疑问句形式，借助助动词did，后面动词用原形read。B项时态颠倒，C项第二空不应使用现在完成时，D项第一空缺少完成时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ won the first prize is the monitor of Class 3.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句关系代词的选择。先行词The boy指人，且在从句中作主语，因此用who。which用于指物；whom用于指人但作宾语；whose表示所属关系，意为'……的'。从句中缺少主语，故选A。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These books ______ to the school library by the students yesterday.
questions.explanation: 本题考查一般过去时的被动语态。主语These books与动词return之间是被动关系，需用被动语态（be+过去分词）；时间状语yesterday表明是一般过去时，因此be动词用were。A项是一般现在时被动，时态不符；C项是主动语态；D项缺少be动词，不是被动结构。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Could you tell me ______?
—Sure. It's on the third floor.
options: A. where is the teachers' office
B. where the teachers' office is
C. where does the teachers' office
D. where the teachers' office does
questions.explanation: 本题考查宾语从句的语序。宾语从句必须使用陈述句语序（主语+谓语），不能倒装。A项是疑问句语序；C、D项结构错误，does位置不对且缺少be动词。只有B项'where the teachers' office is'符合陈述句语序，意为'教师办公室在哪里'。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you are ______ with your current score, you should work harder to get a better one.
questions.explanation: 本题考查形容词词义辨析及语境理解。根据后半句'应该更努力取得更好成绩'可知，前半句意思是'如果你对现在的成绩满意'。be satisfied with是固定搭配，意为'对……满意'。excited（兴奋的）、surprised（惊讶的）、worried（担心的）代入后逻辑不通。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We decided to ______ our old books to the children in mountain areas.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词短语辨析。give away意为'捐赠、赠送'；give up意为'放弃'；give in意为'屈服、让步'；give out意为'分发、耗尽'。根据语境'把旧书给山区孩子'，应选择'捐赠'之意，故选A。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The ______ of this painting is a famous artist from France. (paint)
questions.explanation: 本题考查词性转换。根据句意'这幅画的……是一位来自法国的著名艺术家'，可知空格处需要表示'画家'的名词。paint（绘画）加后缀-er变为painter（画家）。painting意为'画作'，不符合句意；painted是过去分词；paint是动词原形。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an interesting project called "Green Campus". The goal was to help students understand the (8)______ of protecting the environment while improving our school life.

At first, many students were not (9)______ about the project. They thought it would take too much time and bring little change. However, things turned out differently.

Our class decided to (10)______ a recycling program. We collected plastic bottles and waste paper every Friday afternoon. To our surprise, we collected over 200 kilograms of recyclable materials in just one month. The money we got from selling these materials was used to buy books for our school library.

The project not only helped the environment but also taught us valuable lessons. We learned that small actions could make a big (11)______. Now, more and more students are joining us. They bring their used batteries and old clothes to school instead of throwing them away.

This experience has changed the way we think about waste. We realize that everyone can do something to make our world (12)______. I am proud to be part of this meaningful project.
questions.explanation: 本题考查语境推理和名词辨析。根据句意'目标是帮助学生理解保护环境的……'，importance（重要性）最符合语境。the importance of...是固定搭配。result（结果）、reason（原因，常与for搭配）、cause（起因）均不符合此处表达'环保重要性'的语境。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:

At first, many students were not (9)______ about the project. They thought it would take too much time and bring little change. However, things turned out differently.

To our surprise, we collected over 200 kilograms of recyclable materials in just one month. The money we got from selling these materials was used to buy books for our school library.

The project not only helped the environment but also taught us valuable lessons. We learned that small actions could make a big (11)______. Now, more and more students are joining us. They bring their used batteries and old clothes to school instead of throwing them away.

This experience has changed the way we think about waste. We realize that everyone can do something to make our world (12)______. I am proud to be part of this meaningful project.
questions.explanation: 本题考查情感线索和形容词辨析。根据下文'他们认为这会花太多时间且没什么改变'可知，起初学生们对这个项目并不'兴奋/感兴趣'。be excited about意为'对……感到兴奋'。nervous（紧张的）、angry（生气的）、sad（悲伤的）均不符合语境逻辑。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an interesting project called "Green Campus". The goal was to help students understand the (8)______ of protecting the environment while improving our school life.

At first, many students were not (9)______ about the project. They thought it would take too much time and bring little change. However, things turned out differently.

The project not only helped the environment but also taught us valuable lessons. We learned that small actions could make a big (11)______. Now, more and more students are joining us. They bring their used batteries and old clothes to school instead of throwing them away.

This experience has changed the way we think about waste. We realize that everyone can do something to make our world (12)______. I am proud to be part of this meaningful project.
questions.explanation: 本题考查语境推理和动词辨析。根据下文'我们每周五下午收集塑料瓶和废纸'可知，我们班'发起/开始'了一个回收项目。start（开始、发起）符合语境。join（加入）通常指加入已有组织；refuse（拒绝）和stop（停止）与下文积极行动矛盾。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an interesting project called "Green Campus". The goal was to help students understand the (8)______ of protecting the environment while improving our school life.

At first, many students were not (9)______ about the project. They thought it would take too much time and bring little change. However, things turned out differently.

To our surprise, we collected over 200 kilograms of recyclable materials in just one month. The money we got from selling these materials was used to buy books for our school library.

This experience has changed the way we think about waste. We realize that everyone can do something to make our world (12)______. I am proud to be part of this meaningful project.
questions.explanation: 本题考查固定搭配。make a big difference是固定短语，意为'产生重大影响、有很大作用'。句意：我们学到小行动可以产生大影响。make a mistake（犯错）、make a decision（做决定）、make progress（取得进步，不与a连用）均不符合此处固定搭配和语境。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an interesting project called "Green Campus". The goal was to help students understand the (8)______ of protecting the environment while improving our school life.

At first, many students were not (9)______ about the project. They thought it would take too much time and bring little change. However, things turned out differently.

Our class decided to (10)______ a recycling program. We collected plastic bottles and waste paper every Friday afternoon. To our surprise, we collected over 200 kilograms of recyclable materials in just one month. The money we got from selling these materials was used to buy books for our school library.

The project not only helped the environment but also taught us valuable lessons. We learned that small actions could make a big (11)______. Now, more and more students are joining us. They bring their used batteries and old clothes to school instead of throwing them away.

This experience has changed the way we think about waste. We realize that everyone can do something to make our world (12)______. I am proud to be part of this meaningful project.
questions.explanation: 本题考查语境推理和形容词比较级。全文主题是环保项目，因此此处意为'让世界更干净'。cleaner（更干净的）符合环保主题。bigger（更大的）、quieter（更安静的）、busier（更忙的）与回收垃圾、保护环境的语境不符。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Protecting Your Eyes in the Digital Age

In today's world, teenagers spend more time than ever looking at screens. Whether it's for online classes, homework, or entertainment, our eyes are working harder than ever before. Dr. Li Ming, an eye specialist from Shenzhen Hospital, has some useful advice for keeping our eyes healthy.

According to Dr. Li, digital eye strain is becoming increasingly common among students. "When we stare at screens for long periods, our eyes become dry and tired," he explains. "This can lead to headaches and even blurred vision over time."

To solve this problem, Dr. Li suggests following the "20-20-20" rule. Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away (about 6 meters) for at least 20 seconds. This simple habit gives your eye muscles a chance to relax. "It's like taking a short vacation for your eyes," Dr. Li says with a smile.

Besides the 20-20-20 rule, there are other ways to protect your eyes. Make sure your screen is about an arm's length away from your face. The top of the screen should be at or slightly below eye level. Good lighting is also important—avoid working in dark rooms or under strong direct light.

Dr. Li also recommends the "blinking exercise." Normally, we blink about 15 times per minute, but when using screens, this drops to 5 times. Try to blink more often to keep your eyes moist. If your eyes feel dry, you can use artificial tears (眼药水), but don't overuse them.

Remember, your eyes are precious. Once damaged, they are hard to repair. By following these simple tips, you can enjoy technology without hurting your vision.
questions.stem: What is the "20-20-20" rule according to Dr. Li Ming?
options: A. Resting for 20 days after using screens.
B. Looking at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds every 20 minutes.
C. Using screens for no more than 20 minutes every day.
D. Taking a 20-second break after 20 hours of work.
questions.explanation: 本题是细节理解题。根据文章第三段第二句'Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away (about 6 meters) for at least 20 seconds.'可知，20-20-20规则是指每20分钟看20英尺（约6米）外的东西至少20秒。A项20天、C项每天使用不超过20分钟、D项20小时后休息均与原文不符。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
Protecting Your Eyes in the Digital Age

In today's world, teenagers spend more time than ever looking at screens. Whether it's for online classes, homework, or entertainment, our eyes are working harder than ever before. Dr. Li Ming, an eye specialist from Shenzhen Hospital, has some useful advice for keeping our eyes healthy.

According to Dr. Li, digital eye strain is becoming increasingly common among students. "When we stare at screens for long periods, our eyes become dry and tired," he explains. "This can lead to headaches and even blurred vision over time."

To solve this problem, Dr. Li suggests following the "20-20-20" rule. Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away (about 6 meters) for at least 20 seconds. This simple habit gives your eye muscles a chance to relax. "It's like taking a short vacation for your eyes," Dr. Li says with a smile.

Besides the 20-20-20 rule, there are other ways to protect your eyes. Make sure your screen is about an arm's length away from your face. The top of the screen should be at or slightly below eye level. Good lighting is also important—avoid working in dark rooms or under strong direct light.

Dr. Li also recommends the "blinking exercise." Normally, we blink about 15 times per minute, but when using screens, this drops to 5 times. Try to blink more often to keep your eyes moist. If your eyes feel dry, you can use artificial tears (眼药水), but don't overuse them.

Remember, your eyes are precious. Once damaged, they are hard to repair. By following these simple tips, you can enjoy technology without hurting your vision.
questions.stem: What does the word "strain" in Paragraph 2 probably mean?
questions.explanation: 本题是词义猜测题。根据第二段后文解释'When we stare at screens for long periods, our eyes become dry and tired'（长时间盯着屏幕，眼睛会变干和疲劳），可推断strain在此处意为'疲劳、紧张'。A项力量、C项光线、D项疾病均不符合上下文语境。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
Protecting Your Eyes in the Digital Age

In today's world, teenagers spend more time than ever looking at screens. Whether it's for online classes, homework, or entertainment, our eyes are working harder than ever before. Dr. Li Ming, an eye specialist from Shenzhen Hospital, has some useful advice for keeping our eyes healthy.

According to Dr. Li, digital eye strain is becoming increasingly common among students. "When we stare at screens for long periods, our eyes become dry and tired," he explains. "This can lead to headaches and even blurred vision over time."

To solve this problem, Dr. Li suggests following the "20-20-20" rule. Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away (about 6 meters) for at least 20 seconds. This simple habit gives your eye muscles a chance to relax. "It's like taking a short vacation for your eyes," Dr. Li says with a smile.

Besides the 20-20-20 rule, there are other ways to protect your eyes. Make sure your screen is about an arm's length away from your face. The top of the screen should be at or slightly below eye level. Good lighting is also important—avoid working in dark rooms or under strong direct light.

Dr. Li also recommends the "blinking exercise." Normally, we blink about 15 times per minute, but when using screens, this drops to 5 times. Try to blink more often to keep your eyes moist. If your eyes feel dry, you can use artificial tears (眼药水), but don't overuse them.

Remember, your eyes are precious. Once damaged, they are hard to repair. By following these simple tips, you can enjoy technology without hurting your vision.
questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Li Ming's words in the last paragraph?
options: A. We should stop using electronic devices completely.
B. Eye drops can cure all eye diseases.
C. Prevention is more important than treatment for eye health.
D. Only teenagers need to worry about eye strain.
questions.explanation: 本题是推理判断题。根据最后一段'Remember, your eyes are precious. Once damaged, they are hard to repair.'（眼睛很珍贵，一旦受损很难修复），可推断出'预防比治疗更重要'。A项完全停止使用电子设备过于绝对；B项眼药水能治愈所有眼疾与原文'不要过度使用'矛盾；D项只有青少年需要担心与文章开头'teengers'只是例子不符。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
Protecting Your Eyes in the Digital Age

In today's world, teenagers spend more time than ever looking at screens. Whether it's for online classes, homework, or entertainment, our eyes are working harder than ever before. Dr. Li Ming, an eye specialist from Shenzhen Hospital, has some useful advice for keeping our eyes healthy.

According to Dr. Li, digital eye strain is becoming increasingly common among students. "When we stare at screens for long periods, our eyes become dry and tired," he explains. "This can lead to headaches and even blurred vision over time."

To solve this problem, Dr. Li suggests following the "20-20-20" rule. Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away (about 6 meters) for at least 20 seconds. This simple habit gives your eye muscles a chance to relax. "It's like taking a short vacation for your eyes," Dr. Li says with a smile.

Besides the 20-20-20 rule, there are other ways to protect your eyes. Make sure your screen is about an arm's length away from your face. The top of the screen should be at or slightly below eye level. Good lighting is also important—avoid working in dark rooms or under strong direct light.

Dr. Li also recommends the "blinking exercise." Normally, we blink about 15 times per minute, but when using screens, this drops to 5 times. Try to blink more often to keep your eyes moist. If your eyes feel dry, you can use artificial tears (眼药水), but don't overuse them.

Remember, your eyes are precious. Once damaged, they are hard to repair. By following these simple tips, you can enjoy technology without hurting your vision.
questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: B. Ways to Protect Eyes in the Digital Age
C. The Dangers of Electronic Devices
D. How to Use Eye Drops Correctly
questions.explanation: 本题是主旨大意题。文章主要介绍了在数字时代如何保护眼睛的方法，包括20-20-20规则、眨眼练习等。A项李医生的职业生涯只是细节；C项电子设备的危险过于片面且负面；D项如何使用眼药水只是最后提到的一个小建议。B项'数字时代的护眼方法'最能概括全文主旨。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
Balancing Study and Hobbies

Many students find it difficult to balance their schoolwork and hobbies. They often feel stressed when they cannot finish homework on time or give up their favorite activities. (17)______ Here are some useful tips.

First, make a proper schedule. Write down all your tasks and activities for the week. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important deadlines or miss your hobby time.

Second, learn to focus. When studying, put away your phone and other distractions. (19)______ Then you can enjoy your hobbies without worrying about unfinished homework.

Finally, remember that hobbies are not a waste of time. They help you relax and develop new skills. (20)______ Finding the right balance will make your school life more colorful and enjoyable.
options: A. However, with good time management, you can do well in both.
B. For example, you can allocate two hours for homework and one hour for basketball.
C. As a result, you will finish your work more efficiently.
D. In fact, they can improve your study performance in the long run.
E. On the other hand, some students spend too much time on hobbies.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接和过渡句。前文提到学生感到压力大，后文说'这里有一些有用的建议'，因此此处需要转折过渡，表示'然而，通过良好的时间管理，你可以两者兼顾'。A项However表示转折，引出解决方法，符合逻辑。B项是举例，C项是结果，D项进一步说明爱好好处，E项另一方面，均不适合此处承上启下。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Balancing Study and Hobbies

Many students find it difficult to balance their schoolwork and hobbies. They often feel stressed when they cannot finish homework on time or give up their favorite activities. (17)______ Here are some useful tips.

First, make a proper schedule. Write down all your tasks and activities for the week. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important deadlines or miss your hobby time.

Second, learn to focus. When studying, put away your phone and other distractions. (19)______ Then you can enjoy your hobbies without worrying about unfinished homework.

Finally, remember that hobbies are not a waste of time. They help you relax and develop new skills. (20)______ Finding the right balance will make your school life more colorful and enjoyable.
options: A. However, with good time management, you can do well in both.
B. For example, you can allocate two hours for homework and one hour for basketball.
C. As a result, you will finish your work more efficiently.
D. In fact, they can improve your study performance in the long run.
E. On the other hand, some students spend too much time on hobbies.
questions.explanation: 本题考查举例关系。前文说'写下所有任务和活动'，后文说'这样你就不会忘记重要截止日期或错过爱好时间'，因此此处需要具体例子说明如何安排时间。B项'例如，你可以分配两小时做作业，一小时打篮球'是对制定计划的具体举例，符合语境。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Balancing Study and Hobbies

Many students find it difficult to balance their schoolwork and hobbies. They often feel stressed when they cannot finish homework on time or give up their favorite activities. (17)______ Here are some useful tips.

First, make a proper schedule. Write down all your tasks and activities for the week. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important deadlines or miss your hobby time.

Second, learn to focus. When studying, put away your phone and other distractions. (19)______ Then you can enjoy your hobbies without worrying about unfinished homework.

Finally, remember that hobbies are not a waste of time. They help you relax and develop new skills. (20)______ Finding the right balance will make your school life more colorful and enjoyable.
options: A. However, with good time management, you can do well in both.
B. For example, you can allocate two hours for homework and one hour for basketball.
C. As a result, you will finish your work more efficiently.
D. In fact, they can improve your study performance in the long run.
E. On the other hand, some students spend too much time on hobbies.
questions.explanation: 本题考查因果关系。前文说'学习时把手机和其他干扰物收起来'，这是提高效率的方法，因此此处表示结果'因此，你会更高效地完成作业'。As a result表示结果，承接上文的专注行为，引出下文的'然后你可以享受爱好'。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Balancing Study and Hobbies

Many students find it difficult to balance their schoolwork and hobbies. They often feel stressed when they cannot finish homework on time or give up their favorite activities. (17)______ Here are some useful tips.

First, make a proper schedule. Write down all your tasks and activities for the week. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important deadlines or miss your hobby time.

Second, learn to focus. When studying, put away your phone and other distractions. (19)______ Then you can enjoy your hobbies without worrying about unfinished homework.

Finally, remember that hobbies are not a waste of time. They help you relax and develop new skills. (20)______ Finding the right balance will make your school life more colorful and enjoyable.
options: A. However, with good time management, you can do well in both.
B. For example, you can allocate two hours for homework and one hour for basketball.
C. As a result, you will finish your work more efficiently.
D. In fact, they can improve your study performance in the long run.
E. On the other hand, some students spend too much time on hobbies.
questions.explanation: 本题考查递进关系。前文说'爱好帮助你放松和发展新技能'，此处进一步说明爱好的好处'事实上，从长远来看它们能提高你的学习表现'。In fact用于补充说明或强调，符合此处进一步阐述爱好价值的语境。
questions.label: 短文填空
